text.title: LlamaIndex: Index Types and Chunking
text.summary: How to select the right LlamaIndex index type and chunking strategy based on document characteristics and query patterns.

rule.statement: Use VectorStoreIndex as the default for semantic Q&A over document collections; switch to PropertyGraphIndex only when entity relationships are the primary query target.
rule.rationale: VectorStoreIndex supports hybrid search, metadata filtering, and reranking. PropertyGraphIndex (GraphRAG) costs 5-10x more at indexing time due to LLM-based entity extraction — reserve it for legal, medical, or research corpora where relationship traversal is required.

rule.statement: Use KeywordTableIndex for exact-term matching; use SummaryIndex only for small document sets where full-document summarization is the primary task.
rule.rationale: KeywordTableIndex uses BM25-style lookup — effective for product codes, proper nouns, and technical identifiers that embeddings may not capture well. SummaryIndex loads all documents into context, which is impractical above a few dozen documents.

example.code:
from llama_index.core import Settings, VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI from llama_index.embeddings.openai import OpenAIEmbedding
Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini", temperature=0) Settings.embed_model = OpenAIEmbedding(model="text-embedding-3-small") Settings.chunk_size = 512 Settings.chunk_overlap = 50
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./data", recursive=True, required_exts=[".pdf", ".docx", ".md"]).load_data() index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents, show_progress=True)
example.note: Always set Settings explicitly — defaults change between LlamaIndex minor versions and can cause unexpected API costs.

rule.statement: Select the splitter based on document structure: SentenceSplitter for general text, SemanticSplitter for topic-boundary accuracy (+9% recall), MarkdownNodeParser for structured docs, CodeSplitter for source code.
rule.rationale: Fixed-size splitting loses cross-sentence context; semantic chunking preserves topic coherence. The NVIDIA benchmark shows page-level chunking at 0.648 accuracy; semantic chunking improves recall by 9% over fixed-size.

rule.statement: Use HierarchicalNodeParser for long documents that mix high-level context with specific details: embed small child nodes (200 tokens), retrieve parent chunks (1000 tokens) for synthesis via AutoMergingRetriever.
rule.rationale: Small chunks improve retrieval precision; large chunks provide synthesis context. The parent-child pattern gets both without sacrificing either. Merge ratio threshold of 0.5 (merge when >50% of children are retrieved) is a reliable default.

example.code:
from llama_index.core.node_parser import HierarchicalNodeParser, get_leaf_nodes from llama_index.core.retrievers import AutoMergingRetriever from llama_index.core.storage.docstore import SimpleDocumentStore
parser = HierarchicalNodeParser.from_defaults(chunk_sizes=[2048, 512, 128]) nodes = parser.get_nodes_from_documents(documents) leaf_nodes = get_leaf_nodes(nodes)
docstore = SimpleDocumentStore() docstore.add_documents(nodes) from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, StorageContext storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(docstore=docstore) index = VectorStoreIndex(leaf_nodes, storage_context=storage_context)
retriever = AutoMergingRetriever( index.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=12), storage_context=storage_context, simple_ratio_thresh=0.5, )

antipattern.description: Using chunk_size=512 and top_k=2 (LlamaIndex defaults) in production.
antipattern.reason: The defaults are too conservative: top_k=2 misses relevant context; 512 tokens is fine for precision but combine with top_k=5-10 for robust retrieval. Always tune aggressively based on eval metrics before launching.

rule.statement: Use Chroma for local development, Qdrant for production self-hosted, and Pinecone for serverless managed deployments. Match embedding dimensions explicitly between the model and the vector store configuration.
rule.rationale: Mismatched dimensions cause silent ingestion failures. Qdrant provides Rust-level performance, built-in hybrid search, and rich metadata filtering — the best self-hosted option for production. Pinecone auto-scales with no operational burden.
